deep_dive.title: SingGuard-NSFA: Extensible Guardrails for Agentic AI via Generative Reasoning and Real-Time Classification
deep_dive.abstract: We present nsfaguard, a guardrail framework for securing agentic AI systems against operational threats, such as prompt injection, sensitive information extraction, malicious code requests, dangerous tool misuse, and resource exhaustion. We first introduce the NSFA taxonomy, which organizes 185 risk variants into a CIA-triad-grounded hierarchy and is cross-validated against three well-established OWASP guidelines. Based on this taxonomy, we construct a benchmark suite spanning 133 languages, comprising over 93K purpose-built samples targeting both user queries and agent responses, along with 3,435 cross-source samples adapted from five public agent-security datasets. To detect these operational threats in practice, we develop a dual-mode approach combining SFT-based generative reasoning for interpretable offline auditing with discriminative classification heads on the frozen backbone, enabling real-time detection at approximately 50,ms. We release four models with 0.8B, 2B, 4B, and 9B parameters, all achieving $\geq$94% F1 on purpose-built benchmarks and surpassing the strongest competing guardrails by 6 to 12 absolute points. On cross-source evaluation, the 9B model attains 91.29% F1 with a more balanced precision--recall trade-off. Moreover, ablation experiments show that classification heads can equip a guardrail with risk detection capabilities beyond its original scope and achieve state-of-the-art performance. These results demonstrate the extensibility of the approach and its generality as a plug-in enhancement.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: Small guardrail models (0.8B-9B) for real-time safety classification of prompts and responses
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 小参数护栏模型(0.8B-9B)用于实时安全分类
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 小参数护栏模型(0.8B-9B)用于实时安全分类
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 小参数护栏模型(0.8B-9B)用于实时安全分类
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: SingGuard-NSFA proposes dual-mode guardrails with 0.8B-9B parameter models for real-time prompt/response safety classification, achieving >=94% F1 and ~50ms latency, surpassing competing guardrails by 6-12 points.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 现有智能体AI系统缺乏高效可扩展的实时安全护栏。该文提出NSFAGuard框架，构建NSFA风险分类法与多语言基准，并开发双模式护栏：结合SFT生成推理与判别式分类头，在冻结骨干上实现约50ms实时检测。发布0.8B至9B四个小模型，在自建基准上F1超94%，跨源评估9B模型达91.29%，较现有护栏提升6-12个绝对点，验证了小模型护栏的扩展性与实用性。

deep_dive.title: HyperSafe: Inference-Time Safety Recovery for Fine-Tuned Language Models
deep_dive.abstract: Safety alignment in large language models can be fragile under fine-tuning, as even benign task adaptation may increase harmful compliance. Existing defenses mainly follow two directions: they either intervene during or after fine-tuning through retraining or weight modification, which can be costly and may hurt task performance, or they use model-agnostic safety classifiers, which may miss failures specific to a given fine-tuned checkpoint. These limitations motivate a post hoc, model-specific, and non-invasive approach to safety restoration. To meet these requirements, we propose HyperSafe, a framework that restores safety behavior by generating a model-specific Safe Side Network (SSN) for each fine-tuned checkpoint. HyperSafe uses layer-wise activation fingerprints to capture how fine-tuning changes the model's inner representations. With a small set of given calibration prompts, the hypernetwork maps these fingerprints to the parameters of the \ssn{} in a single forward pass. The generated \ssn{} runs alongside the frozen fine-tuned model and performs prompt-level safety classification: harmful prompts are routed to refusal, while safe prompts are answered by the original fine-tuned model. Thus, HyperSafe requires no gradient updates, no safety data at deployment time, and no modification to the deployed model weights. We evaluate HyperSafe on two model families, Qwen2-7B and LLaMA-3-8B, across multiple safety benchmarks. HyperSafe reduces harmful response rates from 19-31% to below 1% on every held-out checkpoint, while keeping downstream task accuracy within 1% of the fine-tuned baseline on average. Code is available at https://github.com/nokronim/project-safety-remedy.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: Training-free safety side network for prompt-level safety classification without modifying model weights
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 无需训练的安全侧网络用于提示级安全分类且不修改模型权重
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 无需训练的安全侧网络用于提示级安全分类且不修改模型权重
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 无需训练的安全侧网络用于提示级安全分类且不修改模型权重
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: HyperSafe generates model-specific Safe Side Networks via a hypernetwork for inference-time safety classification of harmful prompts, requiring no gradient updates or safety data at deployment, reducing harmful rates to below 1%.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: LLM安全对齐在微调后易被破坏，现有防御方法成本高或缺乏模型特异性。该文提出HyperSafe框架，通过超网络基于层激活指纹为微调检查点生成安全侧网络，实现提示级安全分类：有害提示路由至拒绝，安全提示由原模型回答。无需梯度更新、部署时安全数据或修改模型权重，将有害响应率从19-31%降至1%以下，同时保持下游任务精度损失在1%以内。

deep_dive.title: PVDetector: Detecting Prompt Injection Attacks on Purpose-Specific LLM Agents through Policy-Violation Concept Analysis
deep_dive.abstract: Large language models (LLMs) are increasingly deployed as purpose-specific agents to handle domain-specific tasks such as customer service and code generation. These agents are expected to comply with not only generic safety guardrails but also purpose-specific restrictions tailored to their designated roles. Such additional restrictions enlarge the attack surface, particularly to prompt injection (PI) attacks. To defend against such attacks, existing detection methods primarily rely on analyzing input-output patterns, yet yield limited effectiveness. To address this limitation, we turn to analyzing the hidden activation space and discover that LLMs inherently retain latent policy-violation (PV) concepts when prompted with requests beyond their designated purpose. Particularly, PV concepts capture the semantics of conflicts between user queries and predefined restrictions, implicitly reflecting LLMs' intrinsic awareness of recognizing policy violations. Building on this insight, we propose PVDetector, a training-free framework that detects PI attacks during LLM inference by measuring hidden-state alignment with PV concepts, which are derived offline from the contrastive pairs of policy-violating and policy-compliant prompts. Experiments across multiple LLMs and datasets show that PVDetector achieves <1\% false negative rate with minimal auxiliary overhead, consistently outperforming state-of-the-art methods. Our code is available at https://github.com/Claresigle/PVDetector .
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: training-free framework detecting unsafe prompts via hidden-state alignment
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 无训练框架通过隐状态对齐检测不安全提示
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 无训练框架通过隐状态对齐检测不安全提示
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 无训练框架通过隐状态对齐检测不安全提示
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Existing LLM safety detection relying on input-output patterns is limited. PVDetector proposes a training-free framework that detects prompt injection attacks by measuring hidden-state alignment with latent policy-violation concepts derived offline, achieving <1% false negative rate with minimal overhead.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 现有基于输入输出模式的LLM安全检测方法效果有限。PVDetector提出一种无训练框架，通过测量隐状态与离线提取的策略违规概念的对齐度来检测提示注入攻击，在多个LLM和数据集上实现了低于1%的漏报率且辅助开销极小，为轻量级实时安全防护提供了新思路。

deep_dive.title: When Words Are Safe But Actions Kill: Probing Physical Danger Beyond Text Safety in Hidden-State Risk Space
deep_dive.abstract: Large language models (LLMs) increasingly serve as high-level planners for embodied agents, where linguistically benign instructions can become unsafe once grounded in the physical world. We study whether this physically grounded danger is the same safety problem as ordinary text-level content danger. Through hidden-state direction analysis and random-split null tests, we show that content danger (CD) and physical danger (PD) form separable signals in LLM representations across Qwen2.5-3B/7B/14B/32B, Phi-3.5 and SmolLM2. Building on the CD/PD separability, we propose PRISM, a single-layer L2-regularized logistic probe over full hidden states. PRISM achieves 86.2--87.7\% accuracy on SafeAgentBench with 11.7--13.7\% FPR, while same-scale LLM judges over-block safe tasks at 24.7--39.0\% FPR. We further introduce PhysicalSafetyBench-1K (PSB-1K), a contrastive benchmark of 1{,}000 physical-risk pairs without direct harm keywords, to test whether methods detect physically grounded danger rather than explicit unsafe wording. On PSB-1K, PRISM reaches 99.6\% accuracy and 0.7\% FPR, whereas a Qwen2.5-3B judge rejects 67.8\% of safe tasks. PRISM also replicates on SafeText and EARBench, supporting hidden-state probing as a representation-level method for physical safety beyond text moderation.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: Single-layer logistic probe for LLM physical danger classification with low FPR
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 用于LLM物理危险分类的单层逻辑探针，低误报率
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 用于LLM物理危险分类的单层逻辑探针，低误报率
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 用于LLM物理危险分类的单层逻辑探针，低误报率
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: LLMs as embodied planners can produce physically unsafe actions from benign text. PRISM uses a single-layer L2-regularized logistic probe on hidden states to separate content vs physical danger, achieving 86-88% accuracy and low FPR, outperforming LLM judges.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: LLM作为具身智能体规划器时，表面安全的文本可能在物理世界产生危险。本文发现内容危险与物理危险在LLM表征中可分离，提出PRISM——一种基于隐藏状态的单层L2正则化逻辑探针。实验显示PRISM在SafeAgentBench上达86-88%准确率且误报率仅12-14%，远优于同规模LLM裁判的25-39%误报率，为轻量级安全分类提供了有效方法。

quick_skim.title: Data-Efficient Adaptation of LLMs via Attention Head Reweighting
quick_skim.abstract: Learning effectively from limited data is critical in domains like security where labeled examples are scarce. Large language models (LLMs) have demonstrated some capabilities for data-efficient learning, especially through parameter-efficient adaptation methods, but continue to struggle when faced with few samples for difficult tasks. To meet this challenge, we propose Attention Head Reweighting (AHR), a data-efficient method that adapts LLMs to new text-classification tasks by learning only a single scalar per attention head. This drastically reduces the number of parameters that need to be learned by making use of the functional specialization of individual attention heads. Experiments on diverse open-source text classification datasets show that AHR can outperform standard baselines like LoRA when learning from limited samples, despite having 200-1000x fewer trainable parameters, as our AHR only modifies ~0.0001% of the model's parameters. In addition, our learned weights are easy to interpret and can be analyzed to better understand the mechanisms and attention heads responsible for in-context learning abilities in LLMs.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: parameter-efficient LLM adaptation via single scalar per attention head
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 每个注意力头仅学习一个标量的参数高效LLM适配方法
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 每个注意力头仅学习一个标量的参数高效LLM适配方法
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 每个注意力头仅学习一个标量的参数高效LLM适配方法
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Proposes Attention Head Reweighting (AHR), a data-efficient method adapting LLMs to text classification by learning one scalar per attention head, outperforming LoRA with 200-1000x fewer trainable parameters on open-source datasets.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 在安全等标注数据稀缺领域，LLM的参数高效适配方法在少样本困难任务上仍表现不佳。本文提出注意力头重加权（AHR），通过为每个注意力头仅学习一个标量来适配LLM到新文本分类任务，可训练参数比LoRA少200-1000倍。在多种开源文本分类数据集上，AHR在有限样本下优于LoRA等基线，且学到的权重具有可解释性，有助于理解LLM上下文学习机制。该方法为构建轻量安全分类器提供了参数高效的桥接路径。
